rulings.Case Title: FISCAL FUNDING CO. INC. VS. ALLEN MATKIS LEEK GAMBRE MALLORY LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees Incurred On Appeal And In Enforcing Judgment
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, July 16, 2018, Line 1, RESPONDENTS ALAN DONES AND JOHN GUILLORY'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney's Fees Incurred On Appeal And In Enforcing Judgment. Respondents Alan Jones and John Guillory's motion for attorney's fees on appeal and for enforcing judgment is granted. Mr. Jones and Mr. Guillory are entitled to an order awarding them fees and costs in the amount of $81,346.50 against judgment debtors Fiscal Funding Co., Inc. and Calvin Grigsby. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: UNIFUND CCR, LLC VS. MAR, MARTIN T., AKA MARTIN LIU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, July 16, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT MARTIN MAR'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Defendant Martin Mar's motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. The default and default judgment entered against Mr. Mar on November 6, 2013 are set aside. Mr. Mar has shown good cause for setting aside the default and default judgment based on extrinsic fraud as a result of the filing of a proof of service of the summons and complaint on him based on invalid substituted service. Mr. Mar has shown that he did not reside at or have a place of business at the address where he was purportedly substitute served on the date of the purported service and he had no knowledge of this case until September 2017 when his bank account was levied. Mr. Mar acted with reasonable diligence in filing this motion after he learned of the default and default judgment entered against him. Plaintiff Unifund CCR, LLC is required to refund to Mr. Mar $20,542.82, the amount that was taken from Mr. Mar's bank account as a result of a levy on that account by Unifund to collect on the default judgment that has been set aside. Unifund may not make any further efforts to collect on the judgment and all judgment collection documents issued by this court are now invalid. Mr. Mar's request for dismissal of this case due to the failure to serve him within three years of the filing of the case is denied because the time period to serve him is tolled from the date of entry of the default judgment till the date this order is filed. Because Mr. Mar was not validly served, if Unifund wishes to pursue this lawsuit against him, it will need to serve him with a summons and complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Mar is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ARKADY BERGER VS. LOSCH & EHRLICH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment And Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, July 16, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANTS LOSCH & EHRLICH, RONALD LOSCH, MARK MEYER'S Re-Notice Of Defendants Motion For Summary Judgment And Summary Adjudication. Defendants Losch & Ehrlich, Ronald Losch, and Mark Meyer's motion for summary judgement and their alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied in their entirety. There is a triable dispute whether plaintiff Arkady Berger sustained any contract or tort based on (1) the statement in Mr. Berger's declaration that he was not fully credited for sanction awards resulting in his being "under-credited" by approximately $4,500; and (2) defendants did not distribute to Mr. Berger the portion of the interest on the attorney's fees collected that accrued on fees that had been paid by him. There is also a triable dispute whether Mr. Berger gave his informed consent to the right of defendants to collect interest on overdue fees billed to him and thus whether he was damaged in the amount of the interest he was charged. Hernandez v. Siegel (2014) 230 Cal.App.4th 165, 175 is distinguishable from this case. The parties' fee agreement did not provide that no fees will be due until a favorable judgment or settlement is reached, the fees are not statutory, and the policy rationale that "it would make little sense to award interest on a fee award to anyone other than the attorney whose labor remains uncompensated" does not apply when a client pays the attorney on a continuous basis before a fees award is made. While interest on fees awarded in excess of fees that the client has already paid belongs to the attorney, absent a contractual agreement to the contrary, there is triable issue whether Mr. Berger was paid the interests on fees he had already paid. There is also a triable issue whether Mr. Berger gave informed consent to be charged interest on overdue bills given the absence of an interest provision in the fee agreement. Based on defendants' repeated assurances about the accuracy of all calculations and compliance with Mr. Berger's directions as to the trust account, a reasonable juror could infer that defendants had fraudulent intent to support the fraudulent inducement claim. There is also a triable issue whether Mr. Berger had full knowledge of the facts on which his fraud allegations are based. Mr. Berger's statement in his declaration that it was not till May and June 2016 that he became aware that defendants were dishonest in their billing practices precludes summary adjudication on the rescission claim for rescission based on the defense of waiver. Mr. Berger's evidence concerning Mr. Meyer's representations about the judgement, sanctions and interest calculations and his communications regarding the distribution of the funds in the trust account creates a triable issue as to Mr. Meyer's personal liability for fraud, breach of fiduciary duty and conversion. (Shafer v. Berger, Kahn, Shafton, Moss, Figler, Simon & Gladstone (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 54, 68 ("[an] agent or employee is always liable for his own torts, whether his employer is liable or not.")). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Berger is required to prepare a proposed order that complies with CCP 437c(g) and repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GARY KOVACS VS. AVG TECHNOLOGIES USA, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Under California Code Of Civil Procedure 2023.030 For Violations Of The April 2, 2018 Discovery Order; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Monday, July 16, 2018, Line 1, PLAINTIFF GARY KOVACS' Motion For Sanctions Under California Code Of Civil Procedure 2023.030 For Violations Of The April 2, 2018 Discovery Order. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is continued to July 20, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 302 to be heard before Judge Richard Ulmer per ex parte order filed July 10, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK, FSB VS. PEDRO RICO ET AL

Plaintiff American Express Bank, FSB's motion to vacate dismissal and enter judgment is granted. No opposition filed. The dismissal entered on February 8, 2018 is vacated. The Bank is entitled to judgment in its favor and against defendant Pedro Rico in the amount of $19,194.58 plus costs per the memorandum of costs procedure. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the Bank is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment that conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

The matter is on calendar for Monday, July 16, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT BRETT MASTERSON'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Brett Masterson's demurrer to plaintiff Stephen Kavanaugh's first amended complaint is: a) overruled as to the first, fourth and sixth causes of action for negligence, intentional tort and negligence; b) sustained without leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action for premises liability and unfair business practice; and c) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action for fraud. The issue of whether CCP 425.15 applies to the two negligence claims is not amenable to resolution on this demurrer. Section 425.15 provides that an order is necessary to sue "a person serving without compensation as a director or officer of a nonprofit corporation¿" Mr. Masterson fails to identify any allegations or judicially noticeable facts that establish that (1) he is serving without compensation; and (2) the Douglas Manor Homeowners' Association is a nonprofit corporation. The premises liability claims fails because Mr. Kavanaugh does not allege that a dangerous condition of the property caused his alleged injuries. The unfair business practice claims fails because neither Mr. Masterson nor the Douglas Manor Homeowners' Association was engaged in a business practice that affected consumers or competitors. (Dinh Ton That v. Alders Maint. Assn. (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 1419, 1427). The "intentional tort" claim is adequately alleged. The court construes that claim as a battery cause of action. Mr. Kavanaugh is granted leave to amend to allege his fraud claim with more particularity, notwithstanding that the fraud claim appears to be a restatement of the battery claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Mr. Masterson is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

The matter is on calendar for Monday, July 16, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT BRETT MASTERSON'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Brett Masterson's motion to strike portions of plaintiff Stephen Kavanaugh's first amended complaint is denied as to the punitive damages allegations and granted without leave to amend as to the attorney's fees allegations. The battery claim is a sufficient predicate to support Mr. Kavanaugh's request for punitive damages. Mr. Kavanaugh's amended complaint does not identify any statutory, contractual, or legal basis for the recovery of attorney's fees. Moreover, since Mr. Kavanaugh is self-represented, he cannot recover fees. (Gutierrez v. G&M Oil Company, Inc. (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 551, 561.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Mr. Masterson is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREA MANGAN ET AL VS. GILLES DESAULNIERS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, July 16, 2018, Line 10, DEFENDANTS GILLES DESAULNIERS, CALIFORNIA HARVEST COMMISSARY, INC., AND CALIFORNIA HARVEST HEALTHY FOODS RANCH MARKET, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants Gilles Desaulniers, California Harvest Commissary, Inc. and California Harvest Healthy Foods Ranch Market, Inc's demurrer to the first, second and third causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Andrea Mangan and Carole Morton is overruled as to all three causes of action. The dissolution claims are adequately alleged. Per Corporations Code 1800(a) and (b)(4), Mr. Desaulniers' shares are excluded when determining whether Ms. Mangan and Ms. Morton have a sufficient interest for standing because they accuse him of persistent unfairness and fraud. Once Mr. Desaulniers' shares are excluded, Ms. Mangan and Ms. Morton satisfy the one-third requirement. Paragraphs 37 and 40 of the complaint allege that Ms. Mangan and Ms. Morton are excluding Mr. Desaulniers' interest in reaching their calculation and there is no inconsistency. Mr. Desaulniers' reply argument that the fraud or persistent mismanagement is not alleged with the requisite particularity lacks merit. Bauer v. Bauer (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 1106 is not a pleading case and the cases discussing the pleading requirements for a common law fraud claim are inapposite. Paragraphs 12-28 sufficiently allege "persistent unfairness" to support the dissolution claims. Paragraph 15 alleges "misconduct through at least 2015" and paragraph 18 refers to mismanagement and "behavior increasingly erratic well into 2017." The breach of fiduciary duty claim is not facially time-barred. Paragraphs 12-27 allege misconduct through 2017 and the continuous accrual doctrine applies. Ms. Mangan and Ms. Morton sufficiently allege bad faith conduct outside of the business judgment rule. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative ruling in verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY VS. KEYHAN MOHANNA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Keyhan Mohanna Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Monday, July 16, 2018, Line 11, Defendant Keyhan Mohanna's Demurrer To Complaint. Defendant Keyhan Mohanna's demurrer to the sole cause of action for unlawful detainer in the complaint filed by Deutsche Bank National Trust Co. is overruled. Paragraph 3 of the complaint alleges that Mr. Mohanna is "currently in possession of and occupying the property." The court accepts that allegation as true and Mr. Mohanna does not cite portions of the complaint nor judicially noticeable facts that indisputably show that paragraph 3 is false. (Aubry v. Tri-City Hosp. Dist. (1992) 2 Cal.4th 962, 966-967 ("The reviewing court gives the complaint a reasonable interpretation, and treats the demurrer as admitting all material facts properly pleaded.")). Mr. Mohanna has 5 calendar days to answer. (CRC 3.1320(g).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Deutsche Bank is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats the tentative ruling in verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
